skillsAuthorizePage.title: Skills authorization
skillsAuthorizePage.debugDisabled: AI debugging is not enabled yet. Turn it on in Admin Web Settings → Advanced Settings first.
skillsAuthorizePage.oauthModeRequired: OAuth mode is not selected. Switch the auth mode to OAuth in Admin Web Settings → Advanced Settings.
skillsAuthorizePage.invalidOrExpiredLink: This authorization link is invalid or expired. Open a new link from the AI instructions.
skillsAuthorizePage.codeAlreadyExchanged: This authorization code has already been exchanged. Ask the AI to start the authorization flow again.

skillsAuthorizeCard.title: Skills OAuth authorization
skillsAuthorizeCard.description: Click authorize to confirm in a dialog. After approval, the AI must exchange the current authorization code for a key. The backend stores only the hash, and this page never shows the plain key.
skillsAuthorizeCard.confirmIssueDescription: Allow this AI to use Skills (OAuth) for debugging. The authorization lasts 1 hour by default, and only the token hash is stored on the backend.
skillsAuthorizeCard.codeForAi: Authorization code (for the AI to exchange)
skillsAuthorizeCard.exchangeExample: Exchange request example for the AI (code to key)
skillsAuthorizeCard.powershellUtf8Hint: When sending Chinese text or emoji JSON from Windows PowerShell, convert it to UTF-8 bytes first and send it as
skillsAuthorizeCard.powershellUtf8HintSuffix: to avoid it being turned into
skillsAuthorizeCard.verifyLink: After exchanging the key, use it against the verification link:

account.usernamePasswordRequired: Username and password cannot be empty
account.keepAtLeastOneAdmin: At least one admin account must remain
account.fillAllPasswordFields: Please fill in all password fields
account.passwordConfirmMismatch: The new password and confirmation do not match
account.newPasswordMinLength: The new password must be at least 6 characters long
account.confirmDeleteAdminDescription: Delete admin user "{{username}}"? This action cannot be undone.

customStatus.title: Custom Status Configuration
customStatus.description: Set custom display status for device offline or lock-screen states
customStatus.offlineDescription: This status will show when device is completely offline
customStatus.lockDescription: This status will show when device reports LockApp/loginwindow
customStatus.offlineBypassDescription: When any selected device is online, this device is not treated as offline and its offline custom status is hidden.
customStatus.lockBypassDescription: When any selected device is online, this device is not treated as locked and its lock-screen custom status is hidden.
customStatus.noBypassDevicesMatched: No devices match the current search

devices.customStatusDescription: Set custom display status for device offline or lock-screen states
devices.deviceCreatedPending: Device created and pending review because no token is bound
devices.bindingRemovedPending: Binding removed; the device is now pending review
devices.deviceNeedsToken: This device is not bound to a token. Bind one before enabling it
devices.deviceAlreadyReviewed: This device has already been reviewed
devices.bindLaterPending: Do not bind yet (will become pending review)
devices.customIdentityPlaceholder: Leave blank to auto-generate; can match the identity used in Quick Add Activity
devices.customIdentityHint: 8 to 128 characters and must be unique. You can paste the identity generated from “Generate random key” in Overview.
devices.searchPlaceholder: Search by display name or device identity
devices.confirmDeleteDeviceDescription: After deletion, this device identity can no longer report activity.
devices.unboundCannotEnable: Devices without a token cannot be enabled; their reports will go into review.
devices.pinToTopDescription: When enabled, this device stays at the top of the homepage status card before other real-time device updates.
devices.showSteamTitle: Show Steam now playing on status card
devices.showSteamDescription: Uses the site-wide Steam ID from settings. When this device is online and Steam reports a game in progress, the homepage status card shows the current game next to the media info.
devices.reviewDescription: Confirm whether to allow this device. Once approved, it can report activity normally; rejecting it marks it as unavailable. If this is a token rebinding flow, the old token has already been unbound and will switch after your approval.
devices.bindingChangeDetected: A rebinding request was detected: the old token has been unbound and will switch to the current token ({{name}}) after approval.
devices.bindTokenBeforeApprove: Bind a token before clicking Approve.

tokens.createDescription: Create a new API token for reporting activity data
tokens.saveTokenHint: Save the token below now. It is shown only once:
tokens.accessBundle: One-click access bundle (Base64, includes endpoint + key)
tokens.noTokens: No tokens yet. Click “Create Token” to start
tokens.deleteDescription: Delete token "{{name}}"? Devices using this token will no longer be able to report data.
tokens.noRecentDevices: No related devices yet. Devices that reported with this token will appear here.
tokens.qrDescription: A QR code can only be generated right after creation succeeds. After closing the dialog, use the saved Base64 bundle or create a new token.

orphanImages.confirmCleanDescription: This will delete {{count}} unreferenced orphan images. This action cannot be undone.
orphanImages.noOrphanImagesDescription: All assets are currently referenced by inspiration content or covers.
orphanImages.deleteDescription: This will delete the unreferenced orphan image. This action cannot be undone.

addActivity.selectedDeviceHint: The activity will be reported as the selected device. The device name has been prefilled and can still be edited.
addActivity.devicePlaceholderSelected: Prefilled from the device list; editable
addActivity.persistHint: When there is no client report, the activity will be automatically ended on the homepage “Current Status” after this duration (1–1440 minutes, matching the site’s “process stale interval” rule).

setup.title: Site setup
setup.subtitleWithAdmin: Configure the admin account and homepage profile for first use
setup.adminUsernameHint: Used to sign in to the admin panel. Pick something memorable but hard to guess.
setup.adminPasswordHint: Used for admin sign-in. Must be at least 6 characters and should include letters and numbers.
setup.confirmAdminPasswordHint: Enter the admin password again to make sure it is correct.
setup.pageTitleHint: The site title shown in the browser tab, up to {{max}} characters.
setup.userNameHint: The primary name shown to the right of the avatar, such as a nickname or brand name.
setup.userBioHint: A short one-line bio shown below the name.
setup.userBioPlaceholder: Example: Code with patience and optimism.
setup.userNoteHint: Extended text shown below the bio, such as your current status or thoughts.
setup.avatarUrlHint: Paste an image URL directly, or upload and crop one below to generate it automatically.
setup.remoteAvatarFetchTitle: A remote avatar URL was detected. Allow the server to fetch the avatar?
setup.remoteAvatarFetchDescription: When enabled, the homepage and admin preview fetch the avatar through this site instead of letting visitors request the third-party image host directly.
setup.imageUploadHint: After uploading, drag the crop area in the dialog and save a 128×128 square PNG data URL.
setup.avatarPreview: Avatar preview (this will be saved to the database{{fetch}})
setup.historyWindowHint: The homepage history list only shows this recent window. Default: {{minutes}} minutes.
setup.currentSectionHint: The heading shown above the homepage activity detail section.
setup.earlierSectionHint: The heading shown for the second homepage section with recent inspiration entries.
setup.adminEntryHint: Shown on the right side of the footer and opens the admin panel when clicked.
setup.cropAvatarDescription: Drag the crop area or corners to adjust the frame, then generate a 128×128 avatar.
setup.cropSiteIconDescription: Drag the crop area or corners to adjust the frame, then generate a 128×128 site icon.

dashboard.recentRecordsDescription: See what has been happening lately in chronological order.
dashboard.confirmEndActivityDescription: End "{{summary}}" immediately? This non-realtime activity will be removed from the current status right away.

toasts.copiedRulesJson: Rules JSON copied to the clipboard
toasts.importedRulesIntoForm: Rules imported into the current draft.

parseErrors.topLevelMustBeObject: The JSON top level must be an object

appRules.title: App message rules
appRules.showProcessNameTitle: Show process name when a rule matches
appRules.showProcessNameDescription: When enabled, the result becomes "message | process name". When disabled, only the rule text is shown and the original title stays hidden.
appRules.moreRulesHint: {{value}} more rules can be viewed and edited in the dialog.
appRules.dialogDescription: Top-level groups match by process name, then each group may refine by window title using plain text or regex. Matching is case-insensitive by default.
appRules.example: Example: set the process to `Code.exe`, the default text to `Coding`, then add a title rule like `\.tsx$` -> `Writing frontend`.
appRules.groupListDescription: Pick an app first, then edit that app's title rules.
appRules.selectorDescription: Search and choose the app group you want to edit first. Selecting one opens the next dialog for details.
appRules.searchPlaceholder: Search app name, fallback text, title condition, or rule text
appRules.noSearchResults: No matching apps or related rules
appRules.groupEditorDescription: Rules run top-to-bottom: match the process first, then the title subrules.
appRules.defaultTextPlaceholder: Used when no title subrule matches
appRules.defaultTextDescription: If no title subrule matches within the same app, this fallback text is used.
appRules.titleRulesDescription: Split the same app into different states by window title. The first matching rule wins.
appRules.titleRuleModePlainDescription: Matches when the title contains this text, case-insensitively.
appRules.titleRuleModeRegexDescription: Matches the title with a case-insensitive regular expression.
appRules.titleRuleTextDescription: Supports {process} and {title}. Regex matches can also use {match}, {match1}, {match2}, or named captures like {match:name}.
appRules.helpText: Notes: {process} always uses the process name, and {title} uses the already-redacted title. Regex subrules can use {match} for the full match, {match1}/{match2} for capture groups, and {match:name} for named captures. If the app is in “show app name only”, {title} becomes empty.
appRules.emptyEditorDescription: Once created, you can add fallback text and multiple window-title rules for that app.
appRules.invalidRegex: Invalid regex in app {{group}}, title rule {{rule}}: {{message}}

appFilter.title: App visibility filter
appFilter.dialogDescription: Choose blacklist or whitelist mode and maintain the app-name list case-insensitively.
appFilter.blacklistDescription: Apps in the list will be hidden from the current status and history.
appFilter.whitelistDescription: Only apps in the list are shown. If the whitelist is empty, no activity records are shown.
appFilter.blacklistInputDescription: Case-insensitive. Add one app name per line.
appFilter.whitelistInputDescription: Case-insensitive. Only these apps are shown on the public site.
appFilter.whitelistEmpty: Whitelist is empty: no activity is shown publicly

nameOnly.title: Show app name only
nameOnly.dialogDescription: When matched, only the app name is shown and detailed content such as the window title stays hidden.

mediaSource.title: Media source rules (play_source)
mediaSource.dialogDescription: When reported metadata.play_source matches, either hide media information or override the source name shown publicly. Legacy blocklists are treated as block rules.
mediaSource.inputDescription: Enter a source value, lowercase recommended, then choose what happens after a match.

captureReportedApps.title: Save reported app history (for rule selection/export)
captureReportedApps.description: When disabled, no new history records are added, but existing saved data is kept.
captureReportedApps.titleLimitDescription: Keep up to {{max}} recent titles per app and platform. Use 0 to save app names only.

importDialog.title: Import rules JSON
importDialog.description: Import the rules into the current draft. They are only written after you save.
importDialog.label: Rules JSON
importDialog.placeholder: Paste JSON containing version and rules

scheduleManager.configNotLoaded: Settings have not been loaded yet
scheduleManager.saved: Schedule and homepage display settings saved

messages.maxTimeSessionsPerCourse: A single course can contain at most {{value}} time sessions
messages.invalidTimeRange: Session {{index}}: the end time must be later than the start time
messages.selectAtLeastOnePeriod: Please select at least one period
messages.maxPeriodsPerCourse: A single course can contain at most {{value}} periods
messages.invalidSelectedPeriods: The selected periods are invalid. Check the fixed period template.

homeDisplay.title: Homepage display
homeDisplay.description: When enabled, visitors will see the “In class” card to the right of the profile during their own local class time.
homeDisplay.showInClass: Show “In class” on the homepage during class hours
homeDisplay.showNextUpcoming: Show the “Next class” preview between classes
homeDisplay.showLocation: Show class location in the card
homeDisplay.afterClassesLabel: Left-side text after all classes end today
homeDisplay.afterClassesHint: This text replaces the “In class” position when the homepage shows “No more classes today!”. If left blank, it falls back to “Chilling”.

periodTemplate.title: Fixed period template (site-wide)
periodTemplate.description: Courses select periods only instead of entering exact times manually. After editing the template, existing courses update automatically. Drag the left handle to reorder periods within the same part of day.

mobileWeek.emptyDay: No classes scheduled for this day.

courseList.title: Course list
courseList.empty: No courses yet. Add one or import an ICS file.

editor.teacherPlaceholder: Shown only when “Show teacher” is enabled
editor.timeSessionsHint: Enter the start and end time manually. A single course can contain multiple sessions.
editor.selectPeriodsHint: Course times come from the fixed period template. If the template changes, the course time changes automatically.
editor.anchorDateHint: It must match the selected weekday. Changing the weekday automatically snaps the date to the nearest matching day from today onward.

icsImport.title: Import ICS
icsImport.appendWithDuplicateUid: Append (duplicate UIDs will get new ids)

inspirationManager.editingNotice: Editing inspiration #{{id}}. Saving will overwrite the original content and publish it directly.

form.imageHint: The long edge is limited to about {{value}}px and the aspect ratio is freely adjustable
form.attachCurrentStatus: Attach the homepage “Current” status snapshot at submission time
form.contentPlaceholder: Supports basic rich text: bold, italic, lists, code, and more

snapshot.optionalDevice: Optional snapshot device (defaults to the most recently reported device)
snapshot.noDevices: No devices yet. The snapshot will use the most recently reported device.
snapshot.selectActivity: Choose the activity shown in the snapshot (current + recent)
snapshot.noActivityCandidates: No activity candidates are available for this device.
snapshot.selectionHint: The snapshot uses the activity you selected. If you do not pick one, it defaults to the device’s current activity.
snapshot.noActivityDataAfterSubmit: This device has no activity data. The snapshot will be empty after submission.

crop.description: Drag the selection area or its corners to adjust the frame, then export PNG.

search.title: Search records
search.placeholder: Search title, content, or status snapshot

deleteDialog.title: Confirm deletion
deleteDialog.description: Delete this inspiration entry? This action cannot be undone.

platform.title: Platform & access
platform.description: Configure admin capabilities, authentication, and external interfaces.

frontend.title: Frontend display
frontend.description: Control the public site's copy, theme, and visual behavior.

runtime.title: Runtime behavior
runtime.description: Adjust activity display, schedule logic, caching, and reporting rules.

importExport.title: Import & export
importExport.description: Copy the current site bundle or apply a bundle from another environment.

webSettings.adminAppearanceDescription: This affects only the current admin browser and does not change the public site theme.
webSettings.adminThemeModeHint: Switch the admin area between light, dark, or system mode.
webSettings.adminThemeColorHint: Updates admin buttons, highlights, and focus color, then saves with admin settings so it is available on the first paint.
webSettings.adminBackgroundColorHint: Updates the admin page background, cards, and border hierarchy, then saves with admin settings so it is available on the first paint.

remoteAvatar.title: Fetch remote avatar through the server
remoteAvatar.descriptionPrefix: When enabled, remote avatars are proxied through
remoteAvatar.descriptionSuffix: instead of being requested directly from the visitor's browser.

customCss.label: Custom CSS
customCss.hint: Injected after the built-in theme variables. Use this for small overrides instead of replacing the whole theme.

importExport.hint: The exported bundle is Base64-encoded JSON. Importing overwrites only supported site-setting fields.

importDialog.title: Apply site bundle
importDialog.description: Paste an exported bundle below. Supported fields will overwrite the current form state.
importDialog.hint: You still need to save after applying the bundle.
importDialog.invalid: The bundle is invalid or unsupported

toasts.partialSavedHint: Saved changes were synced, and unsaved edits were kept. Please review and retry.

webSettingsBasic.siteIconUrlPlaceholder: Leave empty to use the built-in icon, or paste a URL / Data URL
webSettingsBasic.siteIconUrlHint: Used for the browser tab and bookmark icon. Paste an image URL, a Data URL, or choose a local image and crop it into a square Data URL first.
webSettingsBasic.userNoteSignatureFontTitle: Use a signature font for the note
webSettingsBasic.userNoteSignatureFontDescription: When enabled, the note defaults to Satisfy and falls back to the current UI font for unsupported characters.
webSettingsBasic.userNoteSignatureFontFamilyHint: Optional. Leave empty to use the default Satisfy signature font with the current UI font as fallback.
webSettingsBasic.hitokotoTitle: Use Hitokoto for the homepage note
webSettingsBasic.hitokotoDescriptionPrefix: When enabled, the homepage note can fetch copy from
webSettingsBasic.themePresetHint: Pick a starting point here. Choose Custom Surface below if you want to edit detailed theme tokens.
webSettingsBasic.publicFontsHint: The small expandable control on the public page reads the two font buttons configured here. Each option can use the default stack, Google Fonts, or a custom font file.
webSettingsBasic.publicFontsEnabledDescription: When off, the public page falls back to the default two font buttons. When on, it uses the two options configured below.
webSettingsBasic.avatarUrlHint: Paste an image URL, a Data URL, or upload a file below and crop it first.

webSettingsActivity.globalMouseTiltDescription: Adds a subtle tilt response on supported public cards.
webSettingsActivity.globalMouseTiltGyroDescription: Allow motion sensors on supported mobile devices to drive the tilt effect.
webSettingsActivity.profileOnlinePulseDescription: Adds a pulse animation to the online accent when the profile is currently online.
webSettingsActivity.hideActivityMediaTitle: Hide media details in activity cards
webSettingsActivity.hideActivityMediaDescription: Removes title/artist style media information from public activity cards.
webSettingsActivity.mediaDisplayShowSourceTitle: Show media source in hover card
webSettingsActivity.mediaDisplayShowSourceDescription: When hovering over the music row, display the source (e.g., Spotify, Apple Music) in a popover.
webSettingsActivity.mediaDisplayShowCoverTitle: Show media cover image in hover card
webSettingsActivity.mediaDisplayShowCoverDescription: When hovering over the music row, display the cover image in a popover.
webSettingsActivity.mediaDisplayShowAppIconDescription: When enabled, playback app icons from activity reports are stored and shown before the music row.
webSettingsActivity.mediaDisplayShowNcmLinkDescription: When enabled and the activity report contains genre info (e.g. NCM-{id} from inflink-rs), a link to the song on music.163.com will be shown in the hover card.
webSettingsActivity.mediaCoverMaxCountHint: Number of cover images to keep per device. Set to 0 for unlimited. Older images are automatically deleted.
webSettingsActivity.mediaCoverServerlessWarning: Strongly avoid enabling this on serverless platforms: covers are written to the database with activity reports, which can noticeably increase function payload size, database storage, and read bandwidth. You can ignore this note on non-serverless deployments.
webSettingsActivity.hideInspirationOnHomeDescription: When enabled, the home page hides the inspiration section while keeping the archive and admin content intact.
webSettingsActivity.activityRejectLockappSleepDescription: Filters out Windows LockApp, macOS loginwindow, and similar lock-screen or sleep-state activity reports from the public feed.
webSettingsActivity.forceDisplayTimezoneDescription: When enabled, all formatted schedule and activity times use the timezone selected below instead of the viewer's local timezone.
webSettingsActivity.profileOnlineAccentHint: Choose a custom accent color for the online state, or fall back to the theme default.
webSettingsActivity.profileOnlineAccentInvalid: The online accent color is invalid
webSettingsActivity.displayTimezoneHint: Used when “Force displayed timezone” is enabled.

statusCard.title: Standalone status card
statusCard.descriptionPrefix: Generate an SVG URL that can be used in an
statusCard.descriptionSuffix: tag. The preview uses your current admin session; external access still follows the site lock.
statusCard.enabledDescription: Off by default. After enabling and saving, external callers can read the card from /api/status-card.
statusCard.showHeaderDescription: When enabled, choose whether to show avatar, name, bio, and note.
statusCard.showNoteDescription: The note uses userNote from Basic settings and does not fetch Hitokoto.
statusCard.preferGameDescription: When Steam is currently playing, put the game name in the primary status slot; auto mode also prefers a gaming device.
statusCard.showInClassDescription: When enabled, if the schedule is currently in class and the selected status is sleeping, idle, or lock-screen, the card shows “In class”.
statusCard.deviceKeyWarning: The device identity will be included in the copied URL. Use it only in trusted places; for public embeds, device ID is safer.

webSettingsActivity.updateModeHint: Controls how the public site receives activity updates.

sse.label: SSE push
sse.description: Server-Sent Events balance timeliness and resource usage.

polling.label: HTTP polling
polling.description: Fetches updates on a timer. Uses the fewest server resources but is less real-time.

webSettingsActivity.redisCacheTitle: Use Redis as the secondary activity cache
webSettingsActivity.redisCacheDescription: Keeps public activity snapshots in Redis to help multi-instance deployments stay consistent.
webSettingsActivity.redisCacheServerlessForced: The current deployment environment requires this cache path, so the toggle is locked on.
webSettingsActivity.redisCacheTtlHint: Shorter values refresh more aggressively; longer values reduce cache churn.
webSettingsActivity.steamDescription: Shows the current Steam game in the status card when a supported device is online.
webSettingsActivity.steamIdHintPrefix: You can look up your ID at
webSettingsActivity.processStaleHint: If a process has not reported again after {{value}} seconds by default, it is treated as stale.
webSettingsActivity.autoAcceptNewDevicesDescription: When enabled, newly reporting devices skip manual review and become active immediately.
webSettingsActivity.inspirationDeviceRestrictionDescriptionP: When enabled, only requests carrying an approved

webSettingsCustomSurface.introLine1Prefix: You can use plain CSS image values here. Besides wrapping with
webSettingsCustomSurface.introLine2Prefix: For multi-layer backgrounds, you can still write full
webSettingsCustomSurface.introLine2SuffixPrefix: The final visual result is composited on top of
webSettingsCustomSurface.backgroundSourceHintPrefix: This controls the image source used by

webSettingsCustomSurface.randomApiHintPrefix: The API should return an image response or redirect to one.
webSettingsCustomSurface.randomApiHintSuffix: The preview button resolves the current image once for inspection.
webSettingsCustomSurface.previewEmptyHint: Generate or fetch a preview image first.
webSettingsCustomSurface.livePaletteDescription: Derive theme colors from the current preview image.
webSettingsCustomSurface.backgroundHintPrefix: This feeds theme variables such as
webSettingsCustomSurface.backgroundHintExtendedSuffix: : if the animated background field below is empty, the built-in warm gradient can still cover this area and make it look unchanged.
webSettingsCustomSurface.bodyBackgroundHintSuffix: shorthand (gradients, url(), multiple layers). It is independent from the page background above; leave blank to skip output.
webSettingsCustomSurface.animatedBgHint: Optional decorative layer rendered above the body background.

toasts.duplicatePoolImage: That image already exists in the pool
toasts.paletteFailed: Failed to extract a palette from the preview image
toasts.resolvePreviewFailed: Failed to resolve the preview image

webSettingsHitokoto.pageLoadingDescription: Shows the animated page transition shell between public routes.
webSettingsHitokoto.smoothScrollDescription: When enabled, public pages use Lenis-powered smooth scrolling.
webSettingsHitokoto.searchEngineIndexingDescription: Controls whether the site exposes index-friendly metadata to crawlers.
webSettingsHitokoto.noteTypewriterDescription: Animates the homepage note text when the page first appears.
webSettingsHitokoto.hitokotoTitle: Enable Hitokoto for the homepage note
webSettingsHitokoto.fallbackDescription: If the Hitokoto request fails, keep showing the configured homepage note instead of leaving it blank.

webSettingsOpenApi.title: Expose OpenAPI docs
webSettingsOpenApi.descriptionPrefix: When enabled, the public site serves

webSettingsSecurity.hcaptchaDescriptionSuffix: for admin sign-in and public unlock flows.

webSettingsSkills.enabledDescription: Controls whether this personal life dashboard exposes Skills / MCP tool endpoints and related auth flows for AI clients.
webSettingsSkills.toolModeHint: Choose whether AI clients tune the personal life panel through Skills-style endpoints or the MCP-compatible endpoint set.
webSettingsSkills.apikeyDescription: Generate a single API key an AI client can use to tune status, activity, and display copy.
webSettingsSkills.oauthTtlDescription: Controls how long newly issued OAuth tokens remain valid after exchange.
webSettingsSkills.mdLinkHint: This endpoint returns Markdown that tells AI clients this is a personal life dashboard for status, schedule, activity traces, and inspiration entries.
webSettingsSkills.directLinkHintMiddle: header family. The actual token header is
webSettingsSkills.directLinkHintSuffix: header family. The actual token header is

toasts.copiedLegacyMcpApiKeyUrl: Copied the legacy MCP API-key endpoint URL
toasts.copiedLegacyMcpEndpoint: Copied the legacy MCP endpoint URL

webSettingsSkills.aiAuthDescription: View or revoke issued OAuth authorizations grouped by AI client id.
webSettingsSkills.aiAuthDialogDescription: These records summarize pending codes, approved codes, and exchanged tokens by AI client id.
webSettingsSkills.revokeDialogDescription: This revokes all active OAuth access for the selected AI client id.
webSettingsSkills.mcpEnabledTitle: Enable life-panel tools in MCP mode
webSettingsSkills.mcpEnabledDescriptionPrefix: When enabled, the MCP endpoint exposes theme tool methods controlled by
webSettingsSkills.mcpModeHint: MCP mode uses a dedicated legacy API key flow and a single MCP endpoint for AI clients that can tune the personal life panel.
webSettingsSkills.legacyMcpKeyDescription: Generate or rotate the API key used for the MCP-compatible endpoint.
webSettingsSkills.legacyMcpEndpointHintPrefix: Use this endpoint with a bearer token header like
webSettingsSkills.disabledHint: Enable Skills / MCP first to configure auth modes and expose the personal life panel tooling endpoints.

webSettingsMigration.title: Heavy settings migration

descriptions.legacy: These theme, schedule, rule, and other heavy settings still use the legacy storage path. You can review them now, but you need to migrate before editing.
descriptions.migrated: Heavy settings now use the new split storage. Category editing is enabled, and the old legacy columns are still kept until you clear them.
descriptions.legacy_cleared: The new split storage is active, and the legacy site_config values, old skills authorize-code secrets, and rate_limit_backups cache rows have been cleared. Reads now go straight to the new storage.

confirmMigrate.title: Migrate heavy settings now?
confirmMigrate.description: This copies the legacy heavy settings into the new category tables and switches future reads and writes to the new storage. It does not delete the existing legacy column data.

confirmClear.title: Clear legacy data now?
confirmClear.description: This clears the migrated legacy values in site_config and deletes the old skills_oauth_authorize_code records from system_secrets plus the stale rate_limit_backups cache rows. It does not drop any table structure and does not affect the data already stored in the new tables. This action cannot be automatically undone.

webSettingsMigration.lockedMessage: These settings now belong to the new split storage flow. Run the one-click migration before saving changes here.
webSettingsMigration.lockedToast: Run the one-click migration before editing these heavy settings.

unsavedChanges.revertDialogDescription: Your local edits have not been written to the site settings yet. Are you sure you want to revert them?
